atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum node propagates the configured maximum block tx-list size cap into the payload builder so block construction enforces `max_block_size_bytes`.

destinations.evidence: Closest destination constructor call configures gas limit, blob count, and extra data, but does not pass any block-size cap.
Destination payload-builder config trait no longer exposes `max_block_size_bytes`, so this file cannot forward the downstream setting.

destinations.snippet: pub struct EthereumBuilderConfig { pub desired_gas_limit: u64, pub await_payload_on_missing: bool, pub max_blobs_per_block: Option<u64>, pub extra_data: Bytes }
destinations.evidence: Destination payload builder config removed the `max_block_size_bytes` field entirely, so the downstream block-size enforcement hook has no equivalent target.

atoms.equivalence_argument: No equivalent destination wiring exists. The downstream node-level size cap depends on both a config trait method and a builder-config field that are absent in the v2.2.0 port.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Configure a very small `max_block_size_bytes`, then submit transactions whose combined RLP size exceeds that cap while staying under gas and blob limits; downstream should stop including them, but the destination build path has no size-cap input and would not preserve that behavior.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace the node-to-payload-builder `max_block_size_bytes` propagation so payload construction enforces the downstream transaction-list size cap.
atoms.notes: High risk because it changes payload admission rules and can alter block contents under load.

atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum node forces missing-payload resolution to race an empty payload fallback instead of awaiting the first in-progress payload, preventing consensus-client crashes/stalls when payload building fails.

destinations.evidence: The node builder in the destination never calls `with_await_payload_on_missing(false)`, so it leaves the builder's default missing-payload behavior unchanged.

destinations.snippet: await_payload_on_missing: true ... pub const fn with_await_payload_on_missing(mut self, await_payload_on_missing: bool) -> Self
destinations.evidence: The destination payload builder still supports this toggle, but defaults it to `true`; the downstream override is not propagated from this file.
Because the node wiring does not set the flag to `false`, destination behavior remains `AwaitInProgress` on missing payloads instead of the downstream `RaceEmptyPayload` fallback.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The behavior toggle still exists downstream in the v2.2.0 payload crate, but this file no longer wires the safer fallback. That is a direct behavioral gap, not a rename or structural equivalent.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Induce a payload-build failure or delay so `on_missing_payload` is exercised before any payload is available; downstream should choose `RaceEmptyPayload`, while the destination path will keep `AwaitInProgress` and can reproduce the missing-payload stall/error path.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace the node-level `with_await_payload_on_missing(false)` wiring so missing payloads race an empty fallback instead of awaiting in-progress payloads.
atoms.notes: High risk because it changes the missing-payload state machine on a consensus-critical negative path.

summary: This file carried two downstream payload-builder safety/config propagations, and neither is preserved in the v2.2.0 ported node wiring.
notes: One atom is a full feature removal (`max_block_size_bytes` path absent from config and builder types); the other is an unwired existing toggle that leaves the default missing-payload behavior active.
